87475404.title: Launch Decisions (6/9)
87475404.summary:
## Section 5 — Tech Infrastructure & Architecture
50. **Hosting** — [decided/rec] **Render** for this AI service (`render.yaml` set up); Base44 hosts the app.
51. **Pinecone vs Qdrant** — [rec] Neither at launch (in-house retrieval). At scale: **Qdrant** (self-hostable, fits "closed") over Pinecone (external SaaS).
52. **S3 vs Cloudinary** — [rec] Cloudinary if you want auto-compress out of the box; S3 for full control.
53. **Users in first 3 months** — [your call].
54. **Photo volume** — [your call].
55. **Auto-compress photos** — [rec] Yes.
56. **Push (Firebase)** — [rec] Yes — note: external service.
57. **SMS (Twilio)** — [rec] Yes — external service.
58. **Automated email** — [rec] Yes — external service. (56–58 are the Notifications module, Addendum 2.3.)

72a00a13.title: Missing Modules Addendum (1/4)
72a00a13.summary:
# Scout Fox Go™ — Missing Modules Addendum (Scope of Work)

This addendum documents additional modules required for the complete Scout Fox
Go™ product, incorporated as binding deliverables.

## Required Modules

2.1 Scout Pay™ — Internal Payment Identity: wallet ledger, cashback logic, points redemption, fraud safeguards, UI dashboard.

2.2 Scout Points™ — Rewards Engine: point rules, redemption, leaderboards, AI-triggered bonuses, family pooling.

2.3 Notifications System: push, SMS, email alerts, weather alerts, reminders, smart card scheduling.

dee939a9.title: Missing Modules Addendum (2/4)
dee939a9.summary:
2.4 Family Collaboration System: multi-user trip editing, roles, guest access, shared lists and albums, analytics.

2.5 Scout Guide™: safety library, travel hacks, cultural notes, seasonal content, RAG integration.

2.6 Mood AI System: mood tagging, adaptive itineraries, mood smart cards, tone shaping, stress support.

2.7 RAG Knowledge Base: document ingestion, tagging, versioning, admin upload, vector indexing workflow.

2.8 Scout Scribe™ Full Trip Reports: photo highlights, captioning, story-style summary, themes, export history.

2838a871.title: Missing Modules Addendum (3/4)
2838a871.summary:
2.9 Administrative AI Tools: AI analytics dashboard, session viewer, feedback manager, trace logs, dataset admin.

2.10 Internationalization: Spanish, French, language toggle, multi-language RAG.

2.11 Accessibility Support: high contrast, large text, screen reader compatibility, low-cognitive-load mode.

2.12 Privacy & COPPA Safety: parental verification, minor profile restrictions, deletion rules, AI guardrails.

2.13 Optional Future: VR/AR + 3D Avatar (Phase 3 acknowledgement only).

2.14 Kickstarter & Marketing Tools: referral tracking, supporter badges, creator asset tools.

3c6f09a1.title: Missing Modules Addendum (4/4)
3c6f09a1.summary:
2.15 Smart Cards Automation Engine: weather, mood, rewards, seasonal cards with rule-based scheduling.

## Acceptance Criteria

Deliverables must meet functional, technical, UX, compliance, and performance
standards. Client may request changes if results do not meet module definitions.

612b80b4.title: Launch Decisions (1/9)
612b80b4.summary:
# Scout Fox Go™ — Launch Decisions

Source of truth for the 82-question launch master list. Each answer is tagged:

- **[decided]** — already settled by what's built in this repo (cite the module).
- **[rec]** — engineering recommendation; confirm or override.
- **[your call]** — a business / brand / legal decision that's yours to make.

> This file is also ingested into the closed LMS corpus (`npm run seed:knowledge`
> in `server/`) so Scout can answer team questions about the product itself.

---

289cca9e.title: Launch Decisions (2/9)
289cca9e.summary:
## Section 1 — Product Strategy & Business Model
1. **Core launch features** — [rec] Trip planner (trips/trip_days), Scout Guide (RAG tutor), FamilyCompass recommendations, Mood AI itinerary adaptation, Scout Scribe trip reports, Smart Cards, saved places, packing lists.
2. **Later phases (V2/V3)** — [rec] Scout Pay, full Scout Points economy, family collaboration/real-time co-editing, i18n beyond ES/FR, VR/AR + 3D avatar (Addendum 2.13, explicitly Phase 3).
3. **App Store category** — [rec] **Travel** (primary); Lifestyle as secondary.
4. **Public modules at launch** — [your call] Recommend: Guide, Compass, Trip planner, Scribe, Smart Cards. Keep Pay/Points internal until V2.
5. **Sub-brand logos/colors/icons** — [your call] Recommend one shared identity at launch; sub-brand later.
6. **One Scout Hub vs separate** — [rec] One hub (your Dashboard "Adventure Toolkit" already does this).
7. **Subscription tiers** — [your call] Recommend Free / Pro / Family.
8. **Features per tier** — [your call] Suggest: Free = limited AI credits + core planner; Pro = Advanced model tier + unlimited Guide; Family = multi-profile + collaboration.
9. **Referral discounts / Scout Points bonuses** — [your call] Recommend yes (referral tracking is Addendum 2.14).
10. **Free tier / trial / credits** — [decided/rec] Credit-based AI usage — your `FreemiumUsageTracker` + per-user throttling already fit.
11. **Soft launch / beta** — [your call] Recommend a waitlist beta (waitlist_signups already modeled).
12. **Launch date** — [your call].
13. **Marketing channels** — [your call].
14. **Waitlist landing page** — [rec] Yes; data shape exists (`waitlist_signups`).

634cddcf.title: Launch Decisions (3/9)
634cddcf.summary:
## Section 2 — AI System Design
15. **Scout's personality** — [your call] Recommend: warm, encouraging family sidekick; concise.
16. **Topics to avoid** — [rec] Medical/legal advice, anything unsafe for kids; defer to disclaimers (Q44–46).
17. **Empathetic vs efficient** — [rec] Empathetic-but-brief; lead with the answer.
18. **Proactive alerts** — [rec] Yes via Smart Cards + notifications (2.3/2.15).
19. **Auto-recommend spots** — [decided] FamilyCompass + Mood AI.
20. **Daily Scout Cards** — [decided] Smart Cards engine (`/api/scout/cards/generate`).
21. **Personalization depth** — [decided] Family profiles + learner model drive it.
22. **Long-term preference memory** — [decided] LMS learner model + `family_profiles`.
23. **Family profiles influence recs** — [decided] Wired into Mood AI.
24. **Monthly AI cost target** — [your call] + [rec] tiered models (Fast=Sonnet/Haiku, Pro=Opus) to control it.
25. **Throttle usage** — [rec] Yes, per-user credits.
26. **Cache common answers** — [decided] Closed corpus caches/grounds; research distills back in.
27. **RAG documents** — [decided] Safety tips, travel hacks, cultural briefings, seasonal content (Scout Guide categories, Addendum 2.5).
28. **Content creators/approvers** — [your call] Recommend admin-approved ingestion (KB has source/version/tags).
29. **RAG internal vs external** — [decided] **Internal-only by default; research fallback is opt-in** and writes results back into the closed corpus.

91885a41.title: Launch Decisions (4/9)
91885a41.summary:
## Section 3 — UX, Branding & Content
30. **Mobile/desktop/responsive** — [rec] Mobile-first, responsive (matches the App Store path).
31. **Accessibility** — [your call/rec] High contrast, large text, screen reader, low-cognitive-load mode (Addendum 2.11).
32. **Gamified onboarding** — [your call] Recommend light gamification (ties to Points).
33. **Preloaded destination content** — [rec] Yes; `destinations` seed exists.
34. **Guide includes safety tips + hacks** — [decided] Yes (Scout Guide corpus).
35. **Templates / journals** — [rec] Yes; Scribe + scrapbook cover journaling.
36. **Shared brand palette** — [your call] Recommend yes.
37. **Full brand kit at launch** — [your call].

3a554c45.title: Launch Decisions (5/9)
3a554c45.summary:
## Section 4 — Legal, Safety & Compliance
38. **Parental consent flows** — [your call/legal] Required for under-13 (COPPA, Addendum 2.12).
39. **Kids' own profiles** — [rec] Yes (`family_profiles`/kids_info).
40. **Separate kids' data deletion** — [your call/legal] Yes.
41. **Chat retention** — [rec] 30 days default.
42. **Delete chat history** — [rec] Yes, user-controllable.
43. **GDPR export/delete** — [decided/rec] Yes — your `exportUserData`/`deleteAccount` cover the mechanics.
44. **Travel safety disclaimers** — [your call/legal] Yes.
45. **Weather/logistics accuracy warnings** — [your call/legal] Yes.
46. **Third-party data disclaimers** — [your call/legal] Yes.
47. **Refund policy** — [your call/legal].
48. **Auto-renew** — [your call].
49. **Pause membership** — [your call] Recommend yes.

e970cfda.title: Launch Decisions (7/9)
e970cfda.summary:
## Section 6 — Features & Module Decisions
59. **AI-driven vs user-guided itinerary** — [decided] Hybrid: user trips + AI mood adaptation.
60. **Real-time route optimization** — [your call] V2.
61. **Multi-destination trips** — [your call] Recommend V2.
62. **Static vs AI packing lists** — [decided/rec] Static defaults (`packing_list_items`) + AI augmentation.
63. **Calendar sync** — [your call] V2.
64. **Time-zone adjustments** — [rec] Yes (trips have dates).
65. **Scribe PDFs include photos** — [rec] Yes (scrapbook photos).
66. **Public album sharing** — [your call] Recommend opt-in only (kid safety).
67. **Narrative Trip Stories** — [decided] Scout Scribe.
68. **Multi-user trip collaboration** — [your call] V2 (Addendum 2.4).
69. **Friends join trips** — [your call] V2.
70. **Shared packing lists** — [your call] V2.

2e62d850.title: Launch Decisions (8/9)
2e62d850.summary:
## Section 7 — Admin Panel Decisions
71. **Admin role levels** — [rec] Yes (the Next.js dashboard already assumes roles).
72. **Impersonate users for support** — [your call] Recommend yes, audited.
73. **Admin AI analytics** — [decided] `/api/admin/analytics`, sessions, feedback, traces.
74. **Destination library** — [decided] `destinations` collection + admin tags.
75. **Approve user-created spots** — [rec] Yes, moderation queue.
76. **Destination category tags** — [rec] Yes.

40d04eac.title: Launch Decisions (9/9)
40d04eac.summary:
## Section 8 — Post-Launch Operations
77. **Human customer support** — [your call].
78. **Scout handles basic support** — [rec] Yes — closed tutor over a support KB.
79. **Feedback trains future AI** — [decided] Feedback manager + self-learning loop distill good answers into the corpus.
80. **KPIs** — [rec] WAU, trips created, Guide answer rate, % answered in-house vs research, retention, conversion to paid.
81. **Milestone reports** — [rec] Yes (Scribe + learner milestones).
82. **Analytics dashboard** — [decided] Admin analytics module feeds it.

abc39fbc.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (1/11)
abc39fbc.summary:
# Scout Fox Go™ — Kickstarter Master Plan (Summary for the AI)

Tagline: **Plan Less. Explore More.** Positioning: **The AI Decision Layer for
Human Experiences.** Founder & CEO: Meghan Suslak.

## Executive summary
Scout Fox Go is an AI planning companion for family travel and local experiences.
It sits one layer *above* booking sites — a decision layer that learns who is
traveling and returns a single good plan instead of a long list of options. The
Kickstarter funds finishing the recommendation engine, deepening booking
integrations, hardening security, and launching Scout Points.

938808a7.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (2/11)
938808a7.summary:
## Problem & solution
Family travel planning falls on one person, drowning in options across many
sites, with generic recommendations that ignore real constraints (naps, ages,
budget, accessibility). Scout Fox Go understands the family and hands back a
bookable plan; the user adjusts it conversationally.

33b20b8b.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (3/11)
33b20b8b.summary:
## The Decision Layer (core engine) — request flow
1. **Understand** — parse natural-language input into structured intent
   (destination, dates, budget, pace, who's coming, unspoken constraints).
2. **Gather** — pull live data from booking partners + context (timing, age-fit,
   accessibility, weather, location).
3. **Reason** — score/combine options against the family profile, balancing cost
   vs convenience, ambition vs realism.
4. **Compose** — assemble a coherent plan, structured by day and energy, each
   item tied to a visible reason.
5. **Refine** — conversational feedback reshapes the plan.
Design: modular layers (experience / decision / integration / data+identity);
improves with use; explainable recommendations.

25137acc.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (4/11)
25137acc.summary:
## Product features
Family Profiles, Conversational Planning, Smart Itineraries (by day + energy),
Live Booking Connections, Scout Points Rewards, Local Experiences, Save/Share/
Decide Together.

## User personas
The Default Planner (often a parent/mom carrying it alone), The Time-Starved
Couple, The Multigenerational Organizer, The Local Explorer.

ffe0d4e5.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (5/11)
ffe0d4e5.summary:
## Competitive map
Four existing tool types fail the decision: booking platforms (sell inventory),
search/review (more info), general AI assistants (no live inventory or persistent
profile), travel agents (don't scale). Scout Fox Go fills the gap: agent-level
judgment + platform reach + conversational ease, focused on families.

592ca86a.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (6/11)
592ca86a.summary:
## Booking integrations
**Duffel** (flights), **Kayak** (breadth of search), **PHPtravels** (flexible
booking framework). Each sits behind a consistent internal interface so the
experience is seamless regardless of source. Philosophy: integrate, don't
rebuild inventory/pricing/payments.

## Scout Points (technical)
Central auditable ledger of earn/redeem events; configurable earning rules;
anti-abuse rate limits + validation; partner integrations. Earn: profile
completion, saving plans, booking, referrals, community. Redeem: early access,
upgrades, partner benefits, founding recognition.

b483f0a4.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (7/11)
b483f0a4.summary:
## Security & legal
**FlowVault** = internal security standard: encryption in transit/at rest, data
minimization, access control, privacy by design, extra care for child data
(COPPA-minded). Legal/privacy framework built early; not legal advice.

## Accessibility
Accessible app (contrast, screen reader, clear language) AND accessible *plans*
(step-free routes, age limits, sensory/mobility considerations).

## Business model
Booking-driven revenue (partner economics) + premium membership + Scout Points/
partner ecosystem. Aligned (earn when families get value), diversified,
compounding.

9857dc24.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (8/11)
9857dc24.summary:
## Reward tiers (proposed)
Scout Supporter $15 · Early Explorer $35 · Family Founder $75 · Trip Architect
$150 · Founding Circle $300 · Lifetime Scout $600.

## Stretch goals
Goal: core build. +25% local-experience planning · +50% deeper accessibility in
plans · +75% family sharing/group decisions · +100% expanded Scout Points catalog
+ more booking partners.

## Budget model
Product & engineering 40% · Booking integrations & data 15% · Security &
compliance 10% · Design & UX 10% · Marketing & community 12% · Scout Points &
rewards 5% · Operations & contingency 8%.

e59538a2.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (9/11)
e59538a2.summary:
## 12-month roadmap
Foundation (M1-3): finish engine + integrations, founding-member early access.
Refine (M4-6): sharpen recs, harden security, launch Scout Points v1.
Expand (M7-9): local experiences, family sharing, broader booking, stretch goals.
Scale (M10-12): open beyond founding members, accessibility, premium + growth.

4e2af5c2.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (10/11)
4e2af5c2.summary:
## Launch playbook
Full-funnel marketing (recognition → belief → action → belonging); parenting/
family-travel influencers; pre-launch + launch + mid + final-push email
sequences; warm/real/funny social; PR around launch + milestones; calm skimmable
Kickstarter page; feeling-first video; front-loaded day-by-day timeline.

## Team & ops
Founder-led, lean; own the decision layer / family experience / brand, partner
for inventory + specialized work. Continuous QA (accuracy, fit, reliability,
safety) with real founding families. Mostly-digital fulfillment; communication is
fulfillment.

8d12d6b0.title: Kickstarter Master Plan (11/11)
8d12d6b0.summary:
## Risks & mitigations
Adoption (lead with recognition, build a loyal founding cohort), recommendation
quality (continuous testing, flag uncertainty), partner dependence (multiple
partners behind one interface), competition (focus + trust as the moat), data/
trust (FlowVault), execution/runway (lean, contingency, honest updates).

## Long-term vision
Beyond travel: the trusted decision layer for the hundreds of experience
decisions families make a year — the AI Decision Layer for Human Experiences.
